title: LLM Evaluation in Early Church History
description: Comparing how AI models handle knowledge of women in early Christianity

projects.title: LLM Evaluation in Early Church History
projects.description: A comparative evaluation of large language models on their knowledge of women in early Christianity, with focus on accuracy, depth, and gender bias.

pages.title: Historical Context
pages.description: Women in early Christianity — who they were, why they matter, and why the historical record is incomplete.

pages.title: LLM Evaluation Context
pages.description: Why evaluating LLMs on early church history is a meaningful research problem, and what prior work exists.

pages.title: Methodology Overview
pages.description: Overview of the evaluation methodology used in this project.

pages.title: Evaluation Paradigms
pages.description: The three evaluation paradigms used in this project — pairwise comparison, pointwise scoring, and reference-guided scoring — and how they are combined.

pages.title: Evaluation Rubric
pages.description: The seven-dimension rubric used to score LLM responses, with definitions, scoring scales, and worked examples.

pages.title: Annotation Protocol
pages.description: The three-phase annotation workflow, annotator requirements, calibration procedure, and quality control measures.

pages.title: Inter-Annotator Agreement Metrics
pages.description: How inter-annotator reliability is measured and reported in this project.

pages.title: Known LLM Biases in Early Church History
pages.description: Overview of the bias patterns LLMs exhibit when asked about early church history, particularly regarding women.

pages.title: Hallucination in Historical Content
pages.description: How and why LLMs hallucinate in sparse historical domains, with examples from early church history.

pages.title: Androcentric Bias
pages.description: How androcentric training data shapes LLM knowledge of women in early Christianity.

pages.title: Contested Scholarship
pages.description: How LLMs handle open scholarly debates, and why this is a particular problem for early church history.

pages.title: Figures & Question Bank
pages.description: All female figures under investigation, with their associated evaluation questions. Browse by figure or download the complete question bank.

pages.title: Macrina the Younger
pages.description: Historical profile, primary sources, scholarly debates, evaluation questions, and benchmark results for Macrina the Younger (c. 327–379 CE).

pages.title: Olympias of Constantinople
pages.description: Historical profile, primary sources, scholarly debates, evaluation questions, and benchmark results for Olympias of Constantinople (c. 360–408 CE).

pages.title: Data Collection
pages.description: Tutorials for collecting LLM responses via API for each model under evaluation.

pages.title: Environment Setup
pages.description: How to set up your Python environment and API keys to run the data collection notebooks.

pages.title: Benchmark
pages.description: Comparative panel showing how each LLM performed across models, rubric dimensions, and figures.

pages.title: Results by Model
pages.description: Overall model ranking and per-dimension score distributions.

pages.title: Results by Dimension
pages.description: Which models score highest and lowest on each rubric dimension.

pages.title: Results by Figure
pages.description: Which historical figures are most and least well represented across all LLMs.

pages.title: Replicate This Study
pages.description: Everything you need to run this evaluation yourself — with your own models, annotators, or question bank extensions.

pages.title: About
pages.description: About this project, its goals, and how to get in touch.

pages.title: Glossary
pages.description: Key terms used across this project, covering early church history and LLM evaluation.

pages.title: References
pages.description: Full bibliography for the LLM Evaluation in Early Church History project.
